extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won in a small group sprint after EEKHOFF Nils shut down late attacks, beating MEZGEC Luka easily at 247km.
tactical_patterns: EEKHOFF Nils sacrificed own chances by killing attacks for MATTHEWS, revealing Sunweb prioritized Matthews over own sprinter.
CAVAGNA Rémi's mid-race attack from breakaway forced peloton reorganization but proved too early at 60km-plus out.
GARCÍA CORTINA Iván attempted solo bridge to front group on Côte du Pondoff, showing dangerous late-race range.
Small sprint group formation after 247km brutality favored power-sprinters like MATTHEWS over pure fastmen who got shelled.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won easily in reduced sprint after 247km of attrition, seventh Bretagne Classic attempt yielded first win.
rider_signals.EEKHOFF Nils: positive: Controlled finale for MATTHEWS despite being strongest rider, tactical maturity prioritizing leader over own result.
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: neutral: Second place shows resilience over 247km but lacked sprint speed to challenge MATTHEWS in finale.
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: Junior world champion finished seventh after leading peloton chases, notable endurance for rider age category.
rider_signals.SÉNÉCHAL Florian: neutral: Third place after working for leader shows depth but possibly lacked committed team sprint support.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Attacked from breakaway mid-race showing ambition but timing too early for solo success.
rider_signals.VANHOUCKE Harm: neutral: Featured in late breakaway attempt showing aggression but caught before local lap finale.
